name: 09 — Onboarding Sequence (T+24h / T+72h / T+7d)

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ From: 'hello@mysitehq.com.au', To: $json.email, Subject: $json.subject, HtmlBody: $json.html_body, TextBody: $json.text_body, MessageStream: 'outbound' }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ lead_id: $('Build Email Content').first().json.lead_id, event_type: 'onboarding.email.sent', event_data: { step: $('Build Email Content').first().json.sequence_step, subject: $('Build Email Content').first().json.subject }, source: 'n8n' }) }}

data.jsonData: ={{ JSON.stringify({ lead_id: $('Workflow Input').first().json.lead_id, sequence_step: ($('Workflow Input').first().json.sequence_step || 1) + 1, delay_hours: ($('Workflow Input').first().json.sequence_step || 1) === 1 ? 48 : 96 }) }}

notes: Self-scheduling: each step queues the next via executeWorkflow with delay_hours. Step 1 waits 24h (T+24h post-purchase), step 2 waits 48h more (T+72h), step 3 waits 96h more (T+7d). The workflow exits cleanly if the lead has churned. Requires n8n persistence to be enabled (database-backed, not in-memory) so Wait nodes survive restarts.
